title: Crucix Onboarding Tour
description: Learn the structure and key components of the Crucix intelligence platform

steps.title: Welcome to Crucix
steps.description: Crucix is an intelligence briefing platform that aggregates 27+ data sources in parallel. It synthesizes raw data into actionable insights, detects changes, and alerts via Telegram/Discord. Pure ESM, minimal dependencies, graceful degradation.

steps.title: Entry Points
steps.description: Three ways to run Crucix: server (full dashboard), standalone sweep (CLI), or diagnostics. The server orchestrates the sweep cycle, SSE broadcasting, and alerting.

steps.title: Data Sources Layer
steps.description: The apis/ directory contains 27+ independent source modules. Each exports a briefing function and can run standalone. Sources include FRED (economic), GDELT (news), YFinance (markets), NASA FIRMS (fires), ships (maritime), and more.

steps.title: Core Services
steps.description: lib/ contains three core services: llm/ (6 AI providers via raw fetch), delta/ (change detection engine with hot/cold memory), and alerts/ (Telegram/Discord bots with decay system).

steps.title: Dashboard & UI
steps.description: The dashboard/ directory contains the frontend: jarvis.html (WebGL globe/map HUD), inject.mjs (data synthesizer), and loading.html. Users see live updates via SSE.

steps.title: Configuration
steps.description: crucix.config.mjs controls runtime settings via environment variables. Copy .env.example to .env and configure API keys (FRED, FIRMS, EIA), LLM providers, alerting bots, and refresh intervals.

steps.title: Testing
steps.description: Tests use Node.js built-in test runner. LLM provider tests cover unit and integration tests for OpenRouter and MiniMax in the test/ directory.

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.description: Start the server with npm start, add new sources following CONTRIBUTING.md patterns, or explore lib/llm/ to add LLM providers. Check README.md for full documentation and docker-compose.yml for deployment.
